title: quercus toza var. normalis
intro: Quercus toza var. normalis, also known as Himalayan oak, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Quercus genus of deciduous trees. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The leaves and bark of the Himalayan oak are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
